title: Withholding Zakat
subtitle: Hoarding What Belongs to the Poor, Earning Punishment in the Fire

verses.arabic: وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ بِمَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ هُوَ خَيْرًا لَّهُم ۖ بَلْ هُوَ شَرٌّ لَّهُمْ ۖ سَيُطَوَّقُونَ مَا بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
verses.translation: And let not those who [greedily] withhold what Allah has given them of His bounty ever think that it is better for them; rather, it is worse for them. Their necks will be encircled by what they withheld on the Day of Resurrection.

verses.arabic: وَالَّذِينَ يَكْنِزُونَ الذَّهَبَ وَالْفِضَّةَ وَلَا يُنفِقُونَهَا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَبَشِّرْهُم بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
verses.translation: And those who hoard gold and silver and spend it not in the way of Allah — give them tidings of a painful punishment.

verses.arabic: يَوْمَ يُحْمَىٰ عَلَيْهَا فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ فَتُكْوَىٰ بِهَا جِبَاهُهُمْ وَجُنوبُهُمْ وَظُهُورُهُمْ ۖ هَٰذَا مَا كَنَزْتُمْ لِأَنفُسِكُمْ فَذُوقُوا مَا كُنتُمْ تَكْنِزُونَ
verses.translation: The Day when it will be heated in the fire of Hell and seared therewith will be their foreheads, their flanks, and their backs, [it will be said], 'This is what you hoarded for yourselves, so taste what you used to hoard.'

brief_explanation: The Quran warns in the starkest terms that those who hoard wealth and withhold what Allah has obligated — including zakat — will face a terrifying punishment where their own gold and silver will be heated in Hellfire and branded onto their foreheads, sides, and backs. Zakat is not a favour to the poor but a right belonging to them in the wealth of the affluent, and withholding it is a form of consuming others' rights unjustly. Allah describes the withholder as one who does not merely fail himself but actively wrongs others, for the wealth hoarded was never truly his alone. The Quran frames this greed as a spiritual disease that leads to the Fire.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd (fixed corporal punishment) specific to withholding zakat. However, all four madhabs agree that the ruler (or Islamic state) has the authority to forcibly collect zakat from the one who refuses to pay it, and may impose additional discretionary punishment (ta'zir). The Companions under Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him) went to war against tribes that refused to pay zakat, establishing that its denial is a matter of public order and can be met with force by the state.
